_meta.audit_note: Each entry has been retrieved and read directly. Where a claim could not be traced to a primary source within one step, it has been removed from the site rather than cited to secondary reporting.

sw-charges-26-27.title: How we calculate your bill — 2025-26 and 2026-27 charges
sw-charges-26-27.supports: All tariff figures used on this site. Fetched directly; tables read from page; figures transcribed verbatim.

sw-bills-announcement-26-27.title: Customer bills announcement for 2026/27
sw-bills-announcement-26-27.supports: The 8% average combined bill rise for 2026/27 and the 46.7% rise in 2025/26.

ofwat-pr24-appeals.title: Competition and Markets Authority references — 2024 Price Review
ofwat-pr24-appeals.supports: Regulatory approval of price increases and the appeals by five companies including Southern Water.

cma-pr24-redeterminations.title: Water PR24 price redeterminations
cma-pr24-redeterminations.supports: The CMA final determinations published 26 March 2026 on the disputed PR24 appeals.

ofwat-mfr-2024-25.title: Monitoring Financial Resilience Report 2024-25
ofwat-mfr-2024-25.supports: Sector total borrowings of £82.7 billion at 31 March 2025.

ofwat-mfr-2023-24.title: Monitoring Financial Resilience Report 2023-24
ofwat-mfr-2023-24.supports: Sector net debt of £69.5 billion at 31 March 2024. Dividend policy licence condition effective 2023-24. Sector dividend yield reduced to 3.5%.

sw-dividend-response.title: Our response to media reports on dividends
sw-dividend-response.supports: Southern Water's own statement that no external dividends have been paid since 2017 and that Macquarie has injected more than £1.6 billion of equity since 2021.

ofwat-mfr-2022-23-southern-equity.title: Monitoring Financial Resilience Report 2022-23 — Southern Water equity injection detail
ofwat-mfr-2022-23-southern-equity.supports: £550 million further equity injected into the Southern Water Group in October 2023, of which £375 million received by the regulated entity. £1.1 billion equity injection in September 2021 by Macquarie-managed funds, of which £530 million received by the regulated entity.

ccw.title: Consumer Council for Water
ccw.supports: The statutory independent body for water customer complaints in England and Wales. Free.

watrs.title: Water Redress Scheme
watrs.supports: Independent binding adjudication for unresolved water disputes, accessed via CCW.

sw-complaints.title: Our complaints procedure
sw-complaints.supports: Southern Water's published stage 1 complaints process.

sw-financial-support.title: What if I can't pay my bill?
sw-financial-support.supports: Social tariff offering discounts of at least 45% for eligible customers; Hardship Fund grants and debt write-off.

parliament-find-mp.title: Find your MP
parliament-find-mp.supports: Canonical source for MP details, used via the Members API.

water-uk-supplier-finder.title: Find your supplier
water-uk-supplier-finder.supports: Official postcode-based lookup of water supplier. Used as the fallback for users whose bill is not to hand.

ofwat-2019-penalty.title: PN 21/19: Ofwat confirms Southern Water will pay £126m following investigation
ofwat-2019-penalty.supports: The 2019 enforcement case: £3m penalty plus £123m returned to customers for deliberate misreporting of wastewater performance 2010-2017.

ea-2021-prosecution.title: Record £90 million fine for Southern Water following Environment Agency prosecution
ea-2021-prosecution.supports: 2021 Crown Court fine of £90m for 6,971 illegal sewage discharges between 2010 and 2015.

defra-water-white-paper-2026.title: A new vision for water (White Paper)
defra-water-white-paper-2026.publisher: Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs, GOV.UK
defra-water-white-paper-2026.supports: The government's stated intention to abolish Ofwat and bring water system functions currently split between Ofwat, the DWI, EA and Natural England into a single new regulator. Published 19 February 2026.

boycott-water-bills.title: Boycott Water Bills
boycott-water-bills.publisher: Sick of Sewage / Julie Wassmer, Katy Colley
boycott-water-bills.supports: Existing Kent and Sussex campaign advocating non-payment of the wastewater portion in protest at sewage pollution. Different remit from this site.
